import collections

def edmonds_karp(graph, source, sink):
    num_nodes = len(graph)
    
    residual_graph = [row[:] for row in graph] # Create a deep copy of the graph to represent residual capacities
    
    max_flow = 0
    
    while True:
        parent = [-1] * num_nodes
        path_flow = [0] * num_nodes
        visited = [False] * num_nodes
        
        q = collections.deque()
        q.append(source)
        visited[source] = True
        path_flow[source] = float('inf')
        
        current_path_to_sink_capacity = 0
        
        while q:
            u = q.popleft()
            
            for v in range(num_nodes):
                if not visited[v] and residual_graph[u][v] > 0:
                    visited[v] = True
                    parent[v] = u
                    path_flow[v] = min(path_flow[u], residual_graph[u][v])
                    q.append(v)
                    
                    if v == sink:
                        current_path_to_sink_capacity = path_flow[sink]
                        break
            
            if current_path_to_sink_capacity > 0:
                break

        if current_path_to_sink_capacity == 0:
            break
        
        max_flow += current_path_to_sink_capacity
        
        v = sink
        while v != source:
            u = parent[v]
            residual_graph[u][v] -= current_path_to_sink_capacity
            residual_graph[v][u] += current_path_to_sink_capacity
            v = u
            
    return max_flow
